About: It is about a science fiction board game.
Affects: It affects the city of Sheboygan, Wisconsin.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Japanese kaiju traditions.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the microgame market.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a rectangular grid map of the town, a set of rules and tables, and a counter sheet.
CreatedBy: It was created by Greg Costikyan.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Greg Costikyan.
HasA: It has a rectangular grid map of the town.
It has a set of rules and tables.
It has markers for police, fire fighters, military and unarmed civilians.
It has several different monster counters.
It has damage and fire markers.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by combat-oriented gameplay.
It is characterized by a monster attacking Sheboygan.
It is characterized by one player taking the role of the monster.
It is characterized by the other player taking the role of the city's defenders.
It is characterized by gaining victory points for destroying buildings or eliminating humans.
It is characterized by winning conditions based on victory points or monster destruction.
It is characterized by simple game mechanics.
It is characterized by challenging and interesting gameplay.
It is characterized by a lack of defense against a fire-breath weapon.
It is characterized by humor in its play.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Japanese kaiju traditions.
IsA: It is a science fiction board game.
It is a two-player combat-oriented game.
KnownFor: It is known for its simplicity and speed of play.
It is known for being challenging and interesting.
It is known for its humor.
It is known for providing an opportunity for monstrous fantasies.
PartOf: It is part of Simulations Publications' "Capsule" games.
It is part of SPI's "Space Capsule" games.
It is part of TSR's catalogue.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Simulations Publications.
PublishedAs: It was published as a microgame.
It was published as a "Capsule" game.
It was published as a "Space Capsule" game.
It was published as a boxed set.
It was published as a Polish-language version.
PublishedBy: It was published by Simulations Publications.
PublishedIn: It was published in the UK.
UsedFor: It is used for protecting the city.
It is used for destroying the monster.
It is used for enacting scenarios.
It is used for letting monstrous fantasies run riot.
It is used for dramatizing Japanese monster movies.
UsedIn: It is used in the US District Court in New York.
WonBy: It was won by the monster.
It was won by the humans.
WrittenIn: It was written in four pages of rules.
